songs.title: Lavender Haze
songs.lyrics:
Meet me at midnight

Staring at the ceiling with you
Oh, you don't ever say too much
And you don't really read into
My melancholia

I been under scrutiny (Yeah, oh, yeah)
You handle it beautifully (Yeah, oh, yeah)
All this shit is new to me (Yeah, oh, yeah)

I feel
The lavender haze creeping up on me
Surreal
I'm damned if I do give a damn what people say
No deal
The 1950s shit they want from me
I just wanna stay in that lavender haze

All they keep asking me (All they keep asking me)
Is if I'm gonna be your bride
The only kinda girl they see (Only kinda girl they see)
Is a one-night or a wife

I find it dizzying (Yeah, oh, yeah)
They're bringing up my history (Yeah, oh, yeah)
But you weren't even listening (Yeah, oh, yeah)

I feel
The lavender haze creepin' up on me
Surreal
I'm damned if I do give a damn what people say
No deal
The 1950s shit they want from me
I just wanna stay in that lavender haze

That lavender haze

Talk your talk and go viral
I just need this love spiral
Get it off your chest
Get it off my desk (Get it off my desk)
Talk your talk and go viral
I just need this love spiral
Get it off your chest
Get it off my desk

I feel (I feel)
The lavender haze creeping up on me
Surreal
I'm damned if I do give a damn what people say
No deal (No deal)
The 1950s shit they want from me
I just wanna stay in that lavender haze

Get it off your chest
Get it off my desk
That lavender haze
I just wanna stay

vocab.definition: A state of low spirits or depression, often characterized by a feeling of sadness or hopelessness.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And you don't really read into My melancholia
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her emotional state, implying that she's struggling with feelings of sadness or depression.

vocab.definition: Close and careful examination or observation, often in order to detect flaws or weaknesses.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I been under scrutiny
vocab.context: Taylor is under intense media scrutiny, and she's feeling overwhelmed by the attention.

vocab.definition: Unusual or unexpected, often in a way that's difficult to understand or explain.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm damned if I do give a damn what people say Surreal
vocab.context: Taylor is feeling disconnected from reality, and the pressure from others is making her feel even more disconnected.

vocab.definition: Causing a feeling of dizziness or confusion, often due to a rapid or intense change.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I find it dizzying
vocab.context: Taylor is feeling overwhelmed by the constant questioning and criticism from others.

vocab.definition: Spreading quickly and widely, often through social media or other online platforms.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Talk your talk and go viral
vocab.context: Taylor is feeling frustrated with the way people are talking about her and her relationship, and she wants to set the record straight.

vocab.definition: A situation or process that's becoming increasingly difficult or chaotic, often due to a series of events or actions.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I just need this love spiral
vocab.context: Taylor is feeling overwhelmed by the pressure to conform to societal expectations, and she's struggling to maintain her relationship in the face of all the scrutiny.

songs.title: Maroon
songs.lyrics:
When the morning came
We were cleaning incense off your vinyl shelf
'Cause we lost track of time again
Laughing with my feet in your lap
Like you were my closest friend
"How'd we end up on the floor, anyway?" you say
"Your roommate's cheap-ass screw-top rosé, that's how"
I see you every day now

And I chose you
The one I was dancing with
In New York, no shoes
Looked up at the sky and it was
The burgundy on my t-shirt
When you splashed your wine into me
And how the blood rushed into my cheeks
So scarlet, it was
The mark thеy saw on my collarbone
The rust that grew bеtween telephones
The lips I used to call home
So scarlet, it was maroon

When the silence came
We were shaking, blind and hazy
How the hell did we lose sight of us again?
Sobbing with your head in your hands
Ain't that the way shit always ends?
You were standing hollow-eyed in the hallway
Carnations you had thought were roses, that's us
I feel you, no matter what
The rubies that I gave up

And I lost you
The one I was dancing with
In New York, no shoes
Looked up at the sky and it was (Maroon)
The burgundy on my t-shirt
When you splashed your wine into me
And how the blood rushed into my cheeks
So scarlet, it was (Maroon)
The mark they saw on my collarbone
The rust that grew between telephones
The lips I used to call home
So scarlet, it was maroon

And I wake with your memory over me
That's a real fucking legacy, legacy (It was maroon)
And I wake with your memory over me
That's a real fucking legacy to leave

The burgundy on my t-shirt
When you splashed your wine into me
And how the blood rushed into my cheeks
So scarlet, it was maroon
The mark they saw on my collarbone
The rust that grew between telephones
The lips I used to call home
So scarlet, it was maroon

It was maroon
It was maroon

vocab.definition: a substance that gives off a pleasant smell when burned
vocab.lyric_snippet: We were cleaning incense off your vinyl shelf
vocab.context: Taylor is describing a moment of intimacy and relaxation with her partner, highlighting the use of incense as a calming element.

vocab.definition: a bright red color
vocab.lyric_snippet: So scarlet, it was
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'scarlet' to describe the color of the wine and the flush on her cheeks, emphasizing the intensity of the moment.

vocab.definition: having a vacant or empty look in one's eyes
vocab.lyric_snippet: You were standing hollow-eyed in the hallway
vocab.context: Taylor describes her partner as 'hollow-eyed' to convey a sense of emptiness and sadness, highlighting the emotional state of the relationship.

vocab.definition: a type of flower
vocab.lyric_snippet: Carnations you had thought were roses, that's us
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'carnations' to make a metaphorical comparison between the relationship and a bouquet of flowers, suggesting that the relationship is flawed or imperfect.

vocab.definition: something that is left behind after someone dies or leaves a place
vocab.lyric_snippet: That's a real fucking legacy, legacy
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'legacy' to describe the emotional impact of the relationship on her, suggesting that the memories and emotions left behind are a lasting legacy.

vocab.definition: a reddish-brown substance that forms on metal when it oxidizes
vocab.lyric_snippet: The rust that grew between telephones
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'rust' to describe the decay and disconnection that has developed in the relationship, highlighting the idea that even the smallest things can become a symbol of the relationship's decline.

songs.title: Anti-Hero
songs.lyrics:
I have this thing where I get older, but just never wiser
Midnights become my afternoons
When my depression works the graveyard shift, all of the people
I've ghosted stand there in the room

I should not be left to my own devices
They come with prices and vices
I end up in crisis
(Tale as old as time)
I wake up screaming from dreaming
One day, I'll watch as you're leaving
'Cause you got tired of my scheming
(For the last time)

It's me, hi
I'm the problem, it's me
At teatime, everybody agrees
I'll stare directly at the sun, but never in the mirror
It must be exhausting always rooting for the anti-hero

Sometimes, I feel like everybody is a sexy baby
And I'm a monster on the hill
Too big to hang out, slowly lurching toward your favorite city
Pierced through the heart, but never killed

Did you hear my covert narcissism I disguise as altruism
Like some kind of congressman?
(A tale as old as time)
I wake up screaming from dreaming
One day, I'll watch as you're leaving
And life will lose all its meaning
(For the last time)

It's me, hi
I'm the problem, it's me (I'm the problem, it's me)
At teatime, everybody agrees
I'll stare directly at the sun, but never in the mirror
It must be exhausting always rooting for the anti-hero

I have this dream my daughter-in-law kills me for the money
She thinks I left them in the will
The family gathers 'round and reads it and then someone screams out
"She's laughing up at us from Hell"

It's me, hi
I'm the problem, it's me
It's me, hi
I'm the problem, it's me
It's me, hi
Everybody agrees, everybody agrees

It's me, hi (Hi)
I'm the problem, it's me (I'm the problem, it's me)
At teatime (Teatime), everybody agrees (Everybody agrees)
I'll stare directly at the sun, but never in the mirror
It must be exhausting always rooting for the anti-hero

vocab.definition: the practice of being unselfishly concerned for the welfare of others

vocab.definition: moving or happening in a slow and unsteady way

vocab.definition: secret or hidden, especially in a way that is intended to deceive

vocab.definition: unknown or unacknowledged; not recognized or noticed.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Passing by unbeknownst to me
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a situation where she didn't notice someone passing by, highlighting the idea of being unaware or unnoticed.

vocab.definition: completely disorganized or out of control; in a state of chaos.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm unglued, thanks to you
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her emotional state, implying that she's feeling overwhelmed and out of control due to someone's actions.

vocab.definition: glowing brightly; shining with an inner light; radiant.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I've never seen someone lit from within
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who is shining brightly from within, implying a sense of inner light or radiance.

vocab.definition: the outer area or edge of something; the surrounding region.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Blurring out my periphery

vocab.definition: not honest or truthful; deceitful.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the act of hiding one's true feelings or emotions, implying a sense of dishonesty or deception.

vocab.definition: to bring bad luck or ruin something by speaking or acting in a way that is considered unlucky.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the fear of speaking or acting in a way that might bring bad luck or ruin something.

vocab.definition: a type of spacecraft, typically a small, uncrewed vehicle.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone's eyes, implying a sense of otherworldliness or extraterrestrial origin.

songs.title: You're on Your Own, Kid
songs.lyrics:
Summer went away, still, the yearning stays
I play it cool with the best of them
I wait patiently, he's gonna notice me
It's okay, we're the best of friends
Anyway
I hear it in your voice, you're smoking with your boys
I touch my phone as if it's your face
I didn't choose this town, I dream of getting out
There's just one who could make me stay
All my days

From sprinkler splashes to fireplace ashes
I waited ages to see you there
I search the party of better bodies
Just to learn that you never cared

You're on your own, kid
You always have been

I see the great escape, so long, Daisy May
I picked the petals, he loves me not
Something different bloomed, writing in my room
I play my songs in the parking lot
I'll run away

From sprinkler splashes to fireplace ashes
I called a taxi to take me there
I search the party of better bodies
Just to learn that my dreams aren't rare

You're on your own, kid
You always have been

From sprinkler splashes to fireplace ashes
I gave my blood, sweat, and tears for this
I hosted parties and starved my body
Like I'd be saved by a perfect kiss
The jokes weren't funny, I took the money
My friends from home don't know what to say
I looked around in a blood-soaked gown
And I saw something they can't take away
'Cause there were pages turned with the bridges burned
Everything you lose is a step you take
So, make the friendship bracelets, take the moment and taste it
You've got no reason to be afraid

You're on your own, kid
Yeah, you can face this
You're on your own, kid
You always have been

vocab.definition: a strong and persistent desire for something
vocab.lyric_snippet: still, the yearning stays
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'yearning' to describe a lingering feeling of longing after summer has gone.

vocab.definition: the quality of being patient; calm and quiet in a difficult situation
vocab.lyric_snippet: I wait patiently, he's gonna notice me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'patience' to describe her calm and quiet behavior while waiting for someone to notice her.

vocab.definition: having a pale or unhealthy color, often due to shock or sadness
vocab.lyric_snippet: fireplace ashes
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'ashen' to describe the remains of a fireplace, creating a melancholic atmosphere.

vocab.definition: the physical forms of people, especially when they are attractive or desirable
vocab.lyric_snippet: search the party of better bodies
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bodies' to describe people's physical appearance, highlighting the superficiality of a party.

vocab.definition: the parts of a flower that are usually colorful and delicate
vocab.lyric_snippet: I picked the petals, he loves me not
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'petals' to describe the delicate parts of a flower, symbolizing the fragility of love and relationships.

vocab.definition: past tense of 'bloom', to become or cause something to become more attractive or successful
vocab.lyric_snippet: Something different bloomed, writing in my room
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bloomed' to describe the growth of her creativity and writing skills.

vocab.definition: covered in blood, often due to violence or injury
vocab.lyric_snippet: I looked around in a blood-soaked gown
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'blood-soaked' to describe a dramatic and intense moment in her life, possibly symbolizing emotional pain.

vocab.definition: connections or relationships between people or things
vocab.lyric_snippet: pages turned with the bridges burned
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bridges' to describe the connections she has made and lost in her life, highlighting the pain of losing relationships.

vocab.definition: to experience or enjoy something, especially a pleasant or enjoyable experience
vocab.lyric_snippet: take the moment and taste it
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'taste' to describe the experience of living in the moment and enjoying life.

songs.title: Midnight Rain
songs.lyrics:
Rain
He wanted it comfortable, I wanted that pain
He wanted a bride, I was making my own name
Chasing that fame, he stayed the same
All of me changed like midnight

My town was a wasteland
Full of cages, full of fences
Pageant queens and big pretenders
But for some, it was paradise
My boy was a montage
A slow-motion, love potion
Jumping off things in the ocean
I broke his heart 'cause he was nice
He was sunshine, I was midnight rain

He wanted it comfortable, I wanted that pain
He wanted a bride, I was making my own name
Chasing that fame, he stayed the same
All of me changed like midnight

It came like a postcard
Picture perfect shiny family
Holiday peppermint candy
But for him, it's every day
So I peered through a window
A deep portal, time travel
All the love we unravel
And the life I gave away
'Cause he was sunshine, I was midnight rain

He wanted it comfortable, I wanted that pain
He wanted a bride, I was making my own name
Chasing that fame, he stayed the same
All of me changed like midnight rain
He wanted it comfortable, I wanted that pain
He wanted a bride, I was making my own name
Chasing that fame, he stayed the same
All of me changed like midnight

I guess sometimes we all get
Just what we wanted, just what we wanted
And he never thinks of me
Except for when I'm on TV
I guess sometimes we all get
Some kind of haunted, some kind of haunted
And I never think of him
Except on midnights like this
(Midnights like this, midnights like this)

vocab.definition: a barren, desolate area or place; a state of being without hope or vitality
vocab.lyric_snippet: My town was a wasteland
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wasteland' to describe her hometown as a place lacking vibrancy and hope, setting the tone for her narrative.

vocab.definition: people who falsely claim to have a particular quality or status
vocab.lyric_snippet: Full of cages, full of fences / Pageant queens and big pretenders
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pretenders' to describe people who fake their identities or pretend to be something they're not, highlighting the superficiality of her hometown.

vocab.definition: a place or state of great happiness or beauty
vocab.lyric_snippet: But for some, it was paradise
vocab.context: Taylor contrasts the idea of 'paradise' with the reality of her hometown, suggesting that for some people, the superficiality and emptiness of the town can be appealing.

vocab.definition: a sequence of images or scenes presented in a rapid or disjointed manner
vocab.lyric_snippet: My boy was a montage
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'montage' to describe her former partner as a collection of idealized images or scenes, highlighting the artificial nature of their relationship.

vocab.definition: a picture or message sent to someone as a souvenir of a place or event
vocab.lyric_snippet: It came like a postcard
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'postcard' to describe the idealized image of a perfect family, suggesting that it's a superficial and unrealistic representation of reality.

vocab.definition: a gateway or entrance to another place or state
vocab.lyric_snippet: So I peered through a window / A deep portal, time travel
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'portal' to describe a window as a gateway to the past, highlighting her desire to escape the present and revisit memories.

vocab.definition: to come apart or fall to pieces
vocab.lyric_snippet: All the love we unravel
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'unravel' to describe the process of love falling apart, highlighting the fragility and impermanence of relationships.

songs.title: Vigilante Shit
songs.lyrics:
Draw the cat eye sharp enough to kill a man
You did some bad things, but I'm the worst of them
Sometimes, I wonder which one'll be your last lie
They say looks can kill and I might try

I don't dress for women
I don't dress for men
Lately, I've been dressin' for revenge

I don't start shit, but I can tell you how it ends
Don't get sad, get even
So on the weekends
I don't dress for friends
Lately, I've been dressin' for revenge

She needed cold, hard proof, so I gave her some
She had the envelope, where you think she got it from?
Now she gets the house, gets the kids, gets the pride
Picture me thick as thieves with your ex-wife

And she looks so pretty
Drivin' in your Benz
Lately, she's been dressin' for revenge

She don't start shit, but she can tell you how it ends
Don't get sad, get even
So on the weekends
She don't dress for friends
Lately, she's been dressin' for revenge

Ladies always rise above
Ladies know what people want
Someone sweet and kind and fun

While he was doin' lines and crossin' all of mine

And I don't dress for villains
Or for innocents

I don't start shit, but I can tell you how it ends
Don't get sad, get even
So on the weekends
I don't dress for friends
Lately, I've been dressin' for revenge

vocab.definition: a person who takes the law into their own hands, often by seeking revenge or punishing others
vocab.lyric_snippet: And I don't dress for villains / Or for innocents / I'm on my vigilante shit again
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe her actions, implying she's taking matters into her own hands and seeking revenge.

vocab.definition: people who are not guilty of a crime or wrongdoing
vocab.lyric_snippet: I don't dress for villains / Or for innocents
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to contrast herself with those who are not involved in her revenge plot.

vocab.definition: people who steal or take something without permission
vocab.lyric_snippet: Picture me thick as thieves with your ex-wife
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe her close relationship with her ex-wife, implying they're in cahoots.

vocab.definition: relating to crimes or offenses committed by people in positions of power or respectability
vocab.lyric_snippet: While he was doin' lines and crossin' all of mine / Someone told his white-collar crimes to the FBI
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the type of crimes her ex-partner committed, implying they were serious and respectable.

vocab.definition: very close or intimate, often in a way that's considered unusual or suspicious
vocab.lyric_snippet: Picture me thick as thieves with your ex-wife
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe her close relationship with her ex-wife, implying they're in cahoots.

songs.title: Bejeweled
songs.lyrics:
Baby love, I think I've been a little too kind
Didn't notice you walkin' all over my peace of mind
In the shoes I gave you as a present
Puttin' someone first only works when you're in their top five
And by the way, I'm goin' out tonight

Best believe I'm still bejeweled
When I walk in the room
I can still make the whole place shimmer
And when I meet the band
They ask, "Do you have a man?"
I could still say, "I don't remember"
Familiarity breeds contempt
Don't put mе in the basement
Whеn I want the penthouse of your heart
Diamonds in my eyes
I polish up real, I polish up real nice

Nice

Baby boy, I think I've been too good of a girl (Too good of a girl)
Did all the extra credit, then got graded on a curve
I think it's time to teach some lessons
I made you my world (Huh), have you heard? (Huh)
I can reclaim the land
And I miss you (Miss you), but I miss sparklin' (Nice)

Best believe I'm still bejeweled
When I walk in the room
I can still make the whole place shimmer
And when I meet the band
They ask, "Do you have a man?"
I could still say, "I don't remember"
Familiarity breeds contempt
Don't put me in the basement
When I want the penthouse of your heart
Diamonds in my eyes
I polish up real, I polish up real nice

Nice

Sapphire tears on my face
Sadness became my whole sky
But some guy said my aura's moonstone
Just 'cause he was high
And we're dancin' all night
And you can try to change my mind
But you might have to wait in line
What's a girl gonna do?
A diamond's gotta shine

Best believe I'm still bejeweled
When I walk in the room
I can still make the whole place shimmer (Shimmer)
And when I meet the band
They ask, "Do you have a man?"
I could still say, "I don't remember"
Familiarity breeds contempt
Don't put me in the basement
When I want the penthouse of your heart
Diamonds in my eyes
I polish up real (Nice), I polish up real nice

And we're dancin' all night
And you can try to change my mind
But you might have to wait in line
What's a girl gonna do? What's a girl gonna do?
I polish up nice
Best believe I'm still bejeweled
When I walk in the room
I can still make the whole place shimmer

vocab.definition: covered or decorated with jewels or sparkling objects
vocab.lyric_snippet: Best believe I'm still bejeweled
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bejeweled' to describe her confidence and charisma, implying she still shines and dazzles others.

vocab.definition: to give a faint, sparkling appearance
vocab.lyric_snippet: I can still make the whole place shimmer
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'shimmer' to convey her ability to make a positive impact and create a sense of wonder.

vocab.definition: a feeling of disgust or disdain
vocab.lyric_snippet: Familiarity breeds contempt
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'contempt' to describe the negative emotions that can arise from familiarity and overfamiliarity.

vocab.definition: the top floor of a building, especially one that is luxurious or exclusive
vocab.lyric_snippet: Whеn I want the penthouse of your heart
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'penthouse' to describe her desire for a high level of emotional connection and intimacy.

vocab.definition: to make something shine or look more attractive
vocab.lyric_snippet: I polish up real, I polish up real nice
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'polish' to describe her efforts to present herself in the best possible light and maintain her confidence.

vocab.definition: a feeling or atmosphere that surrounds someone or something
vocab.lyric_snippet: But some guy said my aura's moonstone
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'aura' to describe the impression or energy that she gives off, which can be influenced by external factors.

vocab.definition: a type of gemstone that is often associated with calmness and serenity
vocab.lyric_snippet: But some guy said my aura's moonstone
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'moonstone' to describe the calm and serene impression that she gives off, which is contrasted with her actual emotional state.

vocab.definition: a type of blue gemstone
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sapphire tears on my face
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'sapphire' to describe her tears, which are associated with sadness and emotional pain.

I'll be gettin' over you my whole life

You know how scared I am of elevators
Never trust it if it rises fast
It can't last

Uh-oh, I'm fallin' in love
Oh no, I'm fallin' in love again
Oh, I'm fallin' in love

How'd you turn it right around?

Lost in the labyrinth of my mind

You know how much I hate
That everybody just expects me to bounce back
Just like that

Oh no, I'm fallin' in love again
Oh, I'm fallin' in love
I thought the plane was goin' down
How'd you turn it right around?

I thought the plane was goin' down
How'd you turn it right around?

Oh no, I'm fallin' in love again

Oh no, I'm fallin' in love again
Oh, I'm fallin' in love
I thought the plane was goin' down

vocab.definition: A complex and confusing situation or series of problems.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Lost in the labyrinth of my mind
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe her mental state, feeling overwhelmed and lost.

vocab.definition: To recover quickly from a difficult or unpleasant situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Just like that everybody just expects me to bounce back
vocab.context: Taylor is referring to the expectation that she will quickly recover from a breakup.

vocab.definition: To take air into the lungs and expel it, or to calm down and relax.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Breathe in, breathe through, breathe deep, breathe out
vocab.context: Taylor is using the word to describe a calming and soothing action.

vocab.definition: A device for moving people or goods between floors in a building.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You know how scared I am of elevators
vocab.context: Taylor is using the word to describe a fear or anxiety.

vocab.definition: Feeling unable to cope with a difficult or unpleasant situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Lost in the labyrinth of my mind
vocab.context: Taylor is describing her mental state as feeling overwhelmed and lost.

songs.title: Karma
songs.lyrics:
You're talking shit for the hell of it
Addicted to betrayal, but you're relevant
You're terrified to look down
'Cause if you dare, you'll see the glare
Of everyone you burned just to get there
It's coming back around

And I keep my side of the street clean
You wouldn't know what I mean

'Cause karma is my boyfriend
Karma is a god
Karma is the breeze in my hair on the weekend
Karma's a relaxing thought
Aren't you envious that for you it's not?
Sweet like honey, karma is a cat
Purring in my lap 'cause it loves me
Flexing like a goddamn acrobat
Me and karma vibe like that

Spiderboy, king of thieves
Weave your little webs of opacity
My pennies made your crown
Trick me once, trick me twice
Don't you know that cash ain't the only price?
It's coming back around

And I keep my side of the street clean
You wouldn't know what I mean

'Cause karma is my boyfriend
Karma is a god
Karma is the breeze in my hair on the weekend
Karma's a relaxing thought
Aren't you envious that for you it's not?
Sweet like honey, karma is a cat
Purring in my lap 'cause it loves me
Flexing like a goddamn acrobat
Me and karma vibe like that

Ask me what I learned from all those years
Ask me what I earned from all those tears
Ask me why so many fade, but I'm still here
(I'm still here, I'm still here)

'Cause karma is the thunder
Rattling your ground
Karma's on your scent like a bounty hunter
Karma's gonna track you down
Step by step, from town to town
Sweet like justice, karma is a queen
Karma takes all my friends to the summit
Karma is the guy on the screen
Coming straight home to me

'Cause karma is my boyfriend (Karma is my boyfriend)
Karma is a god
Karma is the breeze in my hair on the weekend (Weekend)
Karma's a relaxing thought
Aren't you envious that for you it's not?
Sweet like honey, karma is a cat
Purring in my lap 'cause it loves me
Flexing like a goddamn acrobat
Me and karma vibe like that

Karma is my boyfriend
Karma is a god (Ah)
Uh-huh, mm
Karma's a relaxing thought

vocab.definition: having or showing a connection or relation to the current situation or issue
vocab.lyric_snippet: Addicted to betrayal, but you're relevant
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'relevant' to describe someone who is still connected to the situation despite their bad behavior

vocab.definition: the state of being unclear or difficult to understand
vocab.lyric_snippet: Weave your little webs of opacity
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'opacity' to describe the unclear or deceitful nature of someone's actions

vocab.definition: a reward offered for the capture or return of someone
vocab.lyric_snippet: Karma's on your scent like a bounty hunter
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bounty' to describe the reward or consequence that karma brings

vocab.definition: the highest or most elevated point of something
vocab.lyric_snippet: Karma takes all my friends to the summit
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'summit' to describe a place of success or achievement

vocab.definition: a gentle wind
vocab.lyric_snippet: Karma is the breeze in my hair on the weekend
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'breeze' to describe the pleasant and effortless nature of karma

vocab.definition: having or showing great skill or agility in physical movements
vocab.lyric_snippet: Flexing like a goddamn acrobat
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'acrobatic' to describe the impressive and effortless nature of karma's actions

vocab.definition: a feeling or atmosphere that is created by someone or something
vocab.lyric_snippet: Me and karma vibe like that
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'vibe' to describe the positive and harmonious feeling she has with karma

vocab.definition: a loud rumbling noise caused by lightning
vocab.lyric_snippet: Karma is the thunder
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'thunder' to describe the powerful and intimidating nature of karma

vocab.definition: a distinctive smell
vocab.lyric_snippet: Karma's on your scent like a bounty hunter
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'scent' to describe the trail or track that karma follows

vocab.definition: a woman who is considered to be the most important or powerful person in a particular field or activity
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sweet like justice, karma is a queen
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'queen' to describe the powerful and authoritative nature of karma

songs.title: Sweet Nothing
songs.lyrics:
I spy with my little tired eye
Tiny as a firefly
A pebble that we picked up last July
Down deep inside your pocket
We almost forgot it
Does it ever miss Wicklow sometimes?
Ooh, ooh

They said the end is comin'
Everyone’s up to somethin'
I find myself runnin' home to your sweet nothings
Outside, they’re push and shovin'
You're in the kitchen hummin'
All that you ever wanted from me was sweet nothin'

On the way home
I wrote a poem
You say, "What a mind"
This happens all the time
Ooh, ooh

'Causе they said the end is comin'
Evеryone's up to somethin’
I find myself runnin’ home to your sweet nothings
Outside, they're push and shovin’
You're in the kitchen hummin'
All that you ever wanted from me was nothin'

Industry disruptors and soul deconstructors
And smooth-talking hucksters out glad-handing each other
And the voices that implore, "You should be doing more"
To you, I can admit that I’m just too soft for all of it
Ooh

They said the end is comin'
Everyone's up to somethin'
I find myself runnin' home to your sweet nothings
Outside, they're push and shovin'
You're in the kitchen hummin'
All that you ever wanted from me was sweet nothin'
They said the end is comin' (They said the end is comin')
Everyone's up to somethin' (Everyone's up to somethin')
I find myself runnin' home to your sweet nothings
Outside, they're push and shovin' (Outside, they're push and shovin')
You're in the kitchen hummin' (You're in the kitchen hummin')
All that you ever wanted from me was sweet nothin'

vocab.definition: feeling or showing a lack of energy or enthusiasm
vocab.lyric_snippet: I spy with my little tired eye
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'tired' to describe her state of mind, implying she's exhausted from the world's expectations.

vocab.definition: people or things that cause significant changes or challenges to established systems or ways of thinking
vocab.lyric_snippet: Industry disruptors and soul deconstructors
vocab.context: Taylor refers to 'disruptors' as external forces that challenge the status quo, contrasting with her desire for a simpler, more intimate life.

vocab.definition: people or things that break down or analyze complex systems or ideas into their component parts
vocab.lyric_snippet: Industry disruptors and soul deconstructors
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'deconstructors' to describe people who scrutinize and analyze complex systems, highlighting her desire to escape such scrutiny.

vocab.definition: people who use clever or deceptive tactics to sell or promote something
vocab.lyric_snippet: And smooth-talking hucksters out glad-handing each other
vocab.context: Taylor describes 'hucksters' as insincere or manipulative individuals who prioritize self-interest over genuine connections.

vocab.definition: to greet or interact with someone in a superficially friendly or flattering way, often in order to gain favor or advantage
vocab.lyric_snippet: And smooth-talking hucksters out glad-handing each other
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'glad-handing' to describe the superficial interactions between self-serving individuals, contrasting with her desire for genuine connection.

vocab.definition: to beg or plead with someone to do something, often in a persistent or urgent manner
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the voices that implore, 'You should be doing more'
vocab.context: Taylor describes 'implore' as the voices of external expectations that pressure her to conform or achieve more.

songs.title: Mastermind
songs.lyrics:
Once upon a time, the planets and the fates
And all the stars aligned
You and I ended up in the same room
At the same time

And the touch of a hand lit the fuse
Of a chain reaction of countermoves
To assess the equation of you
Checkmate, I couldn't lose

What if I told you none of it was accidental?
And the first night that you saw me
Nothing was gonna stop me
I laid the groundwork, and then
Just like clockwork
The dominoes cascaded in a line
What if I told you I'm a mastermind?
And now you're mine
It was all by dеsign
'Cause I'm a mastermind

You see, all the wisеst women
Had to do it this way
'Cause we were born to be the pawn
In every lover's game

If you fail to plan, you plan to fail
Strategy sets the scene for the tale
I'm the wind in our free-flowing sails
And the liquor in our cocktails

What if I told you none of it was accidental?
And the first night that you saw me
I knew I wanted your body
I laid the groundwork, and then
Just like clockwork
The dominoes cascaded in a line
What if I told you I'm a mastermind?
And now you're mine
It was all my design
'Cause I'm a mastermind

No one wanted to play with me as a little kid
So I've been scheming like a criminal ever since
To make them love me and make it seem effortless
This is the first time I've felt the need to confess
And I swear
I'm only cryptic and Machiavellian
'Cause I care

So I told you none of it was accidental
And the first night that you saw me
Nothing was gonna stop me
I laid the groundwork, and then
Saw a wide smirk on your face
You knew the entire time
You knew that I'm a mastermind
And now you're mine
Yeah, all you did was smile
'Cause I'm a mastermind

vocab.definition: moves made in response to an opponent's actions, often to counter or neutralize their advantage.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Of a chain reaction of countermoves
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'countermoves' to describe her strategic actions in response to her partner's moves, showcasing her calculated approach.

vocab.definition: to evaluate or judge the value or quality of something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: To assess the equation of you
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'assess' to describe her evaluation of her partner, indicating she's trying to understand their value or worth.

vocab.definition: to flow or fall in a continuous series of movements or actions.
vocab.lyric_snippet: The dominoes cascaded in a line
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'cascaded' to describe the domino effect of her carefully laid plans, showcasing her ability to orchestrate events.

vocab.definition: a plan or intention, especially one that is carefully thought out and executed.
vocab.lyric_snippet: It was all by design
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'design' to describe her intentional and calculated approach to winning her partner over.

vocab.definition: characterized by cunning, deceit, and a willingness to do whatever it takes to achieve power or success.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm only cryptic and Machiavellian
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'Machiavellian' to describe her calculated and sometimes deceitful approach to achieving her goals.

vocab.definition: difficult to understand or interpret, often because of being ambiguous or obscure.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm only cryptic and Machiavellian
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'cryptic' to describe her sometimes ambiguous or obscure approach to achieving her goals.
